channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How bacteria may promote breast cancer
item.description: Researchers at the Johns Hopkins Kimmel Cancer Center have discovered how certain pathogenic bacteria in gut and breast tissue can promote breast canc...

item.title: Maps can encourage home radon testing in the right settings
item.description: Risk maps for the cancer-causing gas radon can encourage people to test their homes for the substance, but only if homeowners live in known, higher-ri...

item.title: Florida is among states in a 'drug-resistant' salmonella outbreak linked to moringa
item.description: Moringa powder capsules have been recalled as a CDC and FDA investigation links the capsules to a new form of salmonella outbreak that the CDC calls "...

item.title: NSMF protein identified as key regulator in colorectal cancer
item.description: A research team affiliated with UNIST has unveiled a novel approach to halt the progression of colorectal cancer by inducing irreversible cellular agi...

item.title: AI set to make medical scan reports twice as easy to understand for patients
item.description: Artificial intelligence could soon help patients make sense of complex medical scan results, making them far easier to understand without losing clini...

item.title: RHR: Vitamin D: Why a Personalized Approach Is Best
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explains why vitamin D supplementation should never be one-size-fits-all. Drawing on new res...

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain Fog—What’s Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...

item.title: RHR: Seed Oils and Colon Cancer – What the New Research Really Shows
